title: lavandula pedunculata subsp. atlantica
intro: Lavandula pedunculata subsp. atlantica, also known as Spanish lavender, is a subspecies of the lavender plant native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, antispasmodic, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Lavandula pedunculata subsp. atlantica include reducing anxiety and stress, promoting better sleep, and alleviating symptoms of digestive issues and headaches. Lavender preparations can be made into teas, essential oils, and topical creams, which are used to promote relaxation and soothe skin irritations.
